solution: 
```java
class Solution {
    public int countSubstrings(String s) {
        int count = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < s.length(); i++) {
            count += expandAroundCenter(s, i, i); // Odd length palindromes
            count += expandAroundCenter(s, i, i + 1); // Even length palindromes
        }
        return count;
    }

    private int expandAroundCenter(String s, int left, int right) {
        int count = 0;
        while (left >= 0 && right < s.length() && s.charAt(left) == s.charAt(right)) {
            count++;
            left--;
            right++;
        }
        return count;
    }
}
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n^2)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: <The solution uses a technique called expand around the center to efficiently count all palindromic substrings. For each character in the string, it checks for palindromes with both odd and even lengths by expanding around each center. This approach avoids unnecessary recomputation and ensures a quadratic time complexity. The key strength is its efficiency for strings up to length 1000, while the main weakness is that it is not suitable for very large strings due to its O(n^2) time complexity.>
